pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it doesnt even feel like my own anymore. it feels like i'm just a ghost haunting the rooms where we used to be together. i dont see the point in doing any of it anymore... the cooking, the cleaning, just waking up every day. it's all just empty.

pool.baseline: i was just staring at the stars and it hit me... like, what is even the point? the universe is just expanding into nothingness, everything is eventually going to be cold and dead. it makes everything we do—all the pain, all the work, all the trying—just feel like a total joke. if nothing matters in the long run, why are we even bothering to stay?

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very spark of my soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, and the silence of this manor is more deafening than any scream. I feel as though I am merely a ghost haunting my own life, drifting toward a darkness that feels more like home than this cold, empty world ever could. Is there truly any point in enduring the dawn when the night is so much kinder?

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the crash wiped out the whole company and i'm staring at millions in debt that i can never, ever pay back. i can’t even think about going home. the shame of facing my family and my partners after all this... it feels like it's literally suffocating me. i just don't see a way out that doesn't involve total humiliation.
